text.title: django-models Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-models as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Inheriting from models.Model directly instead of the project BaseModel.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Breaks uid/timestamps consistency across the codebase. All domain models must use BaseModel. Audit with: grep -r "models.Model" apps/*/models.py and flag direct inheritance.

failure-mode.description: Omitting full_clean() in service functions before save().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: model.save() does not call full_clean(). Validation defined in clean() or field validators is silently skipped.

failure-mode.description: Exposing id (PK) and uid both in DRF serializers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Defeats the purpose of UUID. Serializers must exclude id; use uid only. DRF viewsets need lookup_field = 'uid'.

failure-mode.description: Using GeneratedField(db_persist=True) or partial indexes on non-PostgreSQL databases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SQLite and MySQL do not fully support these features. Migrations may succeed but fail at query time. Pin the DB engine in a comment next to the field/index.

failure-mode.description: Auto-squashing migrations without reviewing the generated migration file.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Squash can drop UUID unique=True constraints and re-add without backfilling, corrupting production data. Every migration file must be reviewed by a human before migrate runs.

failure-mode.description: Adding db_index=True on every field "for performance" without justification.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every index increases write overhead and storage. Only index fields actually used in filter/order_by/distinct on production traffic.

failure-mode.description: Reordering fields in a model "to clean it up".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Django generates spurious AlterField migrations on field reorder. Lock field order via code review.
